slides.title: Python Talks Back Instantly
slides.body: The **REPL** is a live conversation with Python — type something, press Enter, and Python replies right away. No files, no waiting.
slides.tip: REPL stands for Read-Eval-Print Loop: it reads your input, runs it, prints the result, and loops.

slides.title: The >>> Prompt Is Your Cue
slides.body: Type `python` in your terminal (no filename!) and you'll see `>>>`. That's Python saying: **I'm ready, what do you want to know?**
slides.tip: On some machines you need python3 instead of python.

slides.title: Just Type and Get Answers
slides.body: Any expression you type is evaluated **immediately** — no `print()` required. It works with math, text, and more.

slides.title: Variables Stick Around (For Now)
slides.body: You can create variables in the REPL and use them on the next line. They live until you **close the session** — then they're gone.
slides.tip: Save your work to a .py file if you want to keep it.

slides.title: Explore with dir() and help()
slides.body: `dir(something)` lists everything you can do with it. `help(something)` explains how. Your built-in cheat sheet.
slides.tip: Forget a method name? Type dir([]) to peek inside any object.

slides.title: You've Got a Python Playground
slides.body: The REPL is your **sandbox for experimenting** — try ideas fast, learn by doing, and save real programs to `.py` files. Type `exit()` when you're done.
slides.tip: Great Python developers use the REPL constantly — it's not just for beginners.
